title: How to use BackPricer?
bodyHtml:
BackPricer works 24/7 to optimize your listing selling prices in real-time to make sure you never lose BackBoxes. 

Here’s how it works: Set your minimum and target prices, and BackPricer will find the best possible price for you (based on your quality score), either by hitting your target price or by finding the optimal price between your target price and your minimum price at which you can win the BackBox.


Jump to
How do I activate and deactivate BackPricer?  
How many times per day can I activate or deactivate BackPricer?  
Is BackPricer available on integrations?  
If I use an integration with a re-pricing tool and I want to use BackPricer, will BackPricer override the integration's re-pricing tool?
How should I define my target and minimum prices?  
Is it possible that a bug in BackPricer could send prices spiraling?
What happens when a listing runs out of stock?
Can a seller still win the BackBox even if they’re not the lowest price?  
How does BackPricer interact with the BackBox algorithm?  
Does BackPricer work in real-time?
What happens if I enter a target price that’s too high?
What happens if I enter a minimum price that’s very low?


 

How do I activate and deactivate BackPricer? 

To activate or deactivate BackPricer for all listings, you can use the toggle in your Back Office > “Options” > “BackPricer”.  

When BackPricer is activated, it will apply to all listings where a minimum price and target price are set. When BackPricer is deactivated, the minimum price values of all listings are erased. The target prices remain the only price values.  

To activate BackPricer for a specific listing, simply enter a minimum price. To deactivate BackPricer for a specific listing, delete the minimum price.

 



To activate or deactivate BackPricer through a CSV import:

  • The CSV should contain a “sku” column with the sku reference number of your listing
  • The CSV should contain a “backpricer” column with a “TRUE” or “FALSE” value for each row, specifying if it should be activated for the given listing
  • The CSV should contain a “min_price” column with the min_price value to use for the given listing 
  • If you are in an international context, you should also provide a “lang” column with the country it should be applied to (like “en-us”, “fr-fr”, or “fr-be”)
  • Save your CSV file using the CSV comma-delimited format
  • On the Back Office, go to “import” > “update listing” > “select your file” > “test BackPricer” > “Open” > “Update”
  • You can check that the import was successful using the “Notification” page, or looking directly at your listing page

 


 

To activate BackPricer through an integration, the “min_price” field is available through the following endpoints: 

  • Create listing integration
  • Update specific listing integration
  • Update several listings integration

To get your minimum prices through an integration, the “min_price” field is available through the following endpoints: 

  • Get my listings
  • Get specific listing  

 

How many times per day can I activate or deactivate BackPricer? 

You can activate or deactivate BackPricer for all listings at the same time once a day. Or, you can activate or deactivate BackPricer for a specific listing as often as you want, just by adding or removing the minimum price for that listing.

Note that even without BackPricer activated, you can still win the BackBox by setting the right target price.

 

Is BackPricer available on integrations? 

BackPricer is currently available on SellerCloud and coming soon to other integrations within the next few months.
 
 

If I use an integration with a re-pricing tool and I want to use BackPricer, will BackPricer override the integration's re-pricing tool?

Integrations with re-pricing tools will still be available. The main difference is that an integration’s re-pricing tool is limited to 2 updates per hour, while BackPricer updates prices in real-time. 

 

How should I define my target and minimum prices? 

The target price is the equivalent of the price field when BackPricer is deactivated. You should set your target price to the amount at which you want to sell the listing. You should set your minimum price as low as you’re able to go to win the BackBox without losing money. In this way, you will win more BackBoxes and sell more items, maximizing total revenue.  

The minimum and target price for listings must stay within a maximum range of 8%. This means your target price can’t be more than 8% higher than the corresponding minimum price.

💡 What happens if you go over the limit?

  • Via Back Office: You won’t be able to save the price change, and an error message will pop up.
  • Via Bulk Upload (CSV/API): The BackBox competition tool will automatically adjust your target price to exactly 8% above the minimum price.

To avoid errors and keep your listings performing at their best, please make sure your pricing aligns with this policy. 

Is it possible that a bug in BackPricer could send prices spiraling?

No, this situation can’t happen. The selling price will always be the higher price in the range between the minimum price and the target price, depending on the competition. 

Let’s say seller A and seller B are both selling a product with a target price of 100 €, but seller A’s minimum price is 80 € and seller B’s minimum is 89 €. Who wins the BackBox assuming both sellers have equal quality rates?

Seller A wins the BackBox with a selling price of 88 €. The winner will be the seller with the lowest minimum price, and the selling price will be set just below the minimum price of the closest competitor. To win the BackBox, seller B should set a minimum price of 79 €.



 

What happens when a listing runs out of stock?



If seller A runs out of stock, and there are no other competitors except seller B, then seller B wins the BackBox with a selling price of 100 €. 

The selling price is the higher price in the range between the min price and the target price. Without competition, it is equal to the target price.

 

Can a seller still win the BackBox even if they’re not the lowest price? 

Yes! For example, if seller A adds a minimum price of 400 €, and seller B adds a minimum price of 420 € but has better quality rates than seller A, seller B can win the BackBox. 

 

How does BackPricer interact with the BackBox algorithm? 

The BackBox algorithm remains the same with or without BackPricer. Minimum and target prices set by a seller aren’t the only criteria to win the BackBox — seller and product quality are still taken into account. 

 

Does BackPricer work in real-time? 

Yes! Every time a minimum price or target price is updated by a seller, Back Market recalculates the BackBox winner. 

 

What happens if I enter a target price that’s too high?

If your target and minimum prices are too high, you’ll lose competitiveness and will have less of a chance of winning the BackBox.

If your target price is too high but your minimum price is relevant, you’ll remain competitive, as BackPricer will select the most competitive value compared to the competitors in the range between the minimum price and the target price.

 

What happens if I enter a minimum price that’s very low?

If you enter a minimum price that’s very low but your quality rates are good, you’ll have a high probability of winning the BackBox. Your selling price will be set just below the minimum price of the second competitor, not equal to your own minimum price.

Also, there’s a maximum price range between the minimum and target prices. If a seller accidentally sets a very low price (like 1 €), it will not be taken into account and the BackPricer will be deactivated.
